hGH Fragment (176-191)

The fat-metabolism fragment of growth hormone — without the growth hormone side effects.

AOD-9604 is a small piece of human growth hormone — specifically amino acids 176 through 191. Scientists isolated this fragment because it carries the part of hGH involved in fat metabolism, but not the parts that promote growth or affect blood sugar. That separation is the entire point of this compound.

It works through a completely different pathway than GLP-1 compounds like semaglutide or tirzepatide. Those work through incretin receptors. AOD-9604 comes from the growth hormone family — different origin, different mechanism, different signaling. That makes it useful when the goal is to study how the hGH-derived metabolic pathway functions in isolation.

Why this one matters: GLP-1 peptides (semaglutide, tirzepatide, retatrutide) all work through incretin receptors. AOD-9604 doesn’t touch those receptors at all — it’s the only way to study hGH-related fat metabolism without getting growth signaling involved.
CAS Number
221231-10-3
Molecular Weight
1,815.1 g/mol
Molecular Formula
C₇₈H₁₂₅N₂₃O₂₄S
Appearance
White Lyophilized Powder
What Sets AOD-9604 Apart
Just the fat-metabolism piece — carries the lipolytic activity of hGH without the growth-promoting or blood sugar effects
Completely different pathway — doesn’t touch GLP-1, GIP, or glucagon receptors. Separate mechanism from every incretin compound.
Useful for comparison — study the hGH metabolic pathway side by side with incretin-based approaches to see how they differ
Selective by design — the 176-191 fragment was specifically chosen to isolate metabolic activity from the full hGH molecule
Batch Documentation

Buyer notice & verification standard

The peptide market includes anonymous resellers, short-lived operations, and vendors operating through private Telegram channels using inconsistent, unverifiable, or recycled documentation.

Aūra was built as a long-term alternative in a category where many suppliers appear briefly and disappear just as fast.

We operate under a strict verification standard: independent batch testing, lot-specific Certificates of Analysis, and clearly defined release thresholds. Batches that fail are rejected and never shipped — so labs know exactly what they’re receiving, every time. All orders are discreetly packaged with neutral labeling and documentation to ensure smooth, consistent delivery.

Always quality-tested, verified with third-party COAs

At every stage, Aūra Peptides batches are screened by independent labs for identity, purity, sterility, and endotoxins. Each lot is released only after it meets strict research-grade specifications and is backed by a third-party Certificate of Analysis (COA) for full transparency.

This multi-step process helps ensure that what’s on the label is exactly what’s in the vial—so researchers can focus on data, not doubt.

Identity Test

Passed

Verifies the peptide’s sequence and structure so the correct compound is present in each vial.

Purity Test

Passed

Measures purity and concentration to confirm ≥99% purity on qualifying batches.

Sterility Test

Passed

Screens for bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms to verify sterile handling.

Endotoxicity

Passed

Tests for lipopolysaccharides (LPS) to ensure endotoxin levels stay within research limits.

Why Choose Aūra Peptides

Every Aūra Peptides batch is built around one idea: you should know exactly what's in the vial. Batches are third-party analyzed, COA-backed, and fully traceable so you're not gambling on mystery compounds.

From controlled synthesis partners to batch-specific Certificates of Analysis (COAs), Aūra is designed for teams who want clean, consistent material and repeatable outcomes — not guesswork.

Each lot is produced with vetted synthesis partners and released only after passing third-party identity and purity testing (HPLC, MS or equivalent where appropriate). Batches that don't meet spec simply don't ship.

No mystery blends. Each vial is clearly labeled with peptide name, strength, lot ID, and form (lyophilized powder or solution). Batch COAs are available for every peptide so you can document exactly which material was used in each project.

Products are packaged, labeled, and shipped as research-only reference materials. All Aūra Peptides products are supplied strictly for laboratory research use only — not for human or animal consumption, medical treatment, or diagnostic procedures.

Lots are checked for appearance and practical handling characteristics such as solubility and stability under recommended storage conditions, so you spend less time troubleshooting reagents and more time running your work.

*Aūra Peptides products are sold strictly for laboratory research use only. Not for human or animal use, or any diagnostic or therapeutic application.*

You ask,
we answer.

Straightforward answers to key questions researchers commonly ask — verification, COAs, storage, and handling for research use only.

Each Aūra Peptides lot is evaluated using analytical methods such as HPLC and mass spectrometry (where applicable) to confirm identity and purity before release.

Yes — every peptide includes a batch-specific COA reporting identity confirmation, purity data, and analytical results for full research documentation.

Most Aūra peptides are supplied as lyophilized (freeze-dried) material in sealed vials labeled with peptide name, amount, and lot number for precise tracking.

Research-grade quality is standard — our peptides target ≥98% purity by HPLC unless otherwise noted. Exact purity values are listed on each COA.

Lyophilized peptides should be stored sealed, protected from moisture and light, and kept refrigerated or frozen according to the COA or product guidelines.

After reconstitution for research use, peptides are typically stored aliquoted under refrigerated or frozen conditions to maintain stability and minimize freeze–thaw cycles.

Shelf life varies by peptide, but unopened lyophilized vials stored under recommended conditions remain stable long-term. Refer to the COA for peptide-specific stability data.
